meta.reportLabel: Sun Report — RBNZ hike bets COLLAPSE (OIS <30% from ~70%): Wed hold near-certain, binary defused, trade the guidance; JPY follow-through holding into tonight's thin Asia open (prime ambush tape); Fed Sept ~50% drifting to October; Iran vows Hormuz fees 'definitely' post-60-day window - a dated late-Aug tail.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: The weekend reprice is in Wellington: OIS collapsed to under 30% for a Jul 8 RBNZ hike (from ~70%) as the oil slide vs the RBNZ's ~$100 May assumption guts July urgency - a hold is near-certain and Wednesday becomes a guidance event (September lift-off is the bank-economist base case). JPY holds its confirmed follow-through (past 161, Katayama backing) with Sunday/Monday thin tape prime ambush territory; USD's hike tail keeps deflating (Sept ~50%, October drift) against a zero-cuts floor; the oil glut (WTI ~$68.5-69, Brent ~$71-72, Saudi at 90% of baseline) keeps CAD weakest. New dated tail: Iran vows Hormuz fees 'definitely' once the 60-day free window lapses (~late Aug).
meta.nextBigEvent: Wed Jul 8: RBNZ (hold near-certain - trade the guidance) + Fed June minutes; tonight's Asia open is the first thin tape since the JPY follow-through confirmed

dailyRead: The weekend's biggest reprice happened in Wellington: RBNZ hike bets have COLLAPSED - OIS now prices under 30% of a July 8 hike (from ~70% Friday and 80%+ a week ago), as the oil slide (spot near $70 vs the ~$100 assumed in the RBNZ's May projections) guts the near-term inflation case; a hold Wednesday now looks near-certain, with ASB/Westpac/ANZ all pointing to a September lift-off. The Wednesday binary is defused - NZD event risk shifts from coin-flip to guidance-reading. Elsewhere the board is unchanged from Friday's close: JPY holds its confirmed follow-through (past 161, two straight days of gains, Katayama's 'ready at any time' on the record) with tonight's thin Asia tape prime ambush territory; USD's hike tail keeps deflating (Sept ~50% and drifting, some desks now pushing the first hike to October) while zero cuts priced still floors it; and CAD stays weakest on the intact glut (WTI ~$68.5-69, Brent ~$71-72, Saudi exports back to 90% of the pre-war baseline). New slow-burn tail: Iran said July 4 it will 'definitely' charge Hormuz fees once the 60-day free-transit window lapses - a dated collision point with Washington after the funeral ends July 9. Cleanest board: USD/CAD and AUD/CAD longs, plus the armed JPY shorts.

strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss, unemp 4.2%; Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, zero 2026 cuts priced; Sept hike odds ~50% and drifting, some desks push the first hike to October; Warsh Jul 1: 'this committee will deliver price stability.'
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC but the hike tail keeps deflating (Sept ~50%, October drift); the zero-cut floor holds USD up while the NFP miss removed the motor. Jun CPI (~Jul 14) either re-arms the tail or confirms the crack. Risk: a second soft labor print unwinds stretched longs. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); quiet weekend, thesis unchanged. Next BoE Jul 30 + MPR.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike-tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: tonight's thin Asia open = ambush turf
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Friday's close locked the signal: past 161, two straight days of gains, Katayama 'ready to intervene at any time.'
strength.forward: 🦅 Follow-through holding into the new week; tonight's post-holiday thin Asia tape is exactly where the no-warning MoF regime operates - long-JPY adds justified, trail stops rather than pyramid. Risk: a full April-style retrace (the Y11.7tn round had two good days too); the ~250bp+ carry gap. Next BoJ Jul 30-31.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0% (RBA sees a 4.8% Q2 peak); unemp 4.5% loosening. RBA 4.35% (hiked May), held Jun; market prices ~4.70% by year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky trim + year-end pricing keeps an Aug 11 hike tail open; AUD capped sub-0.70 by the oil-glut hit to commodity beta. Risk: cool Q2 jobs/CPI or risk-off flushes the beta. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: CPI 3.1% Q1 (RBNZ sees >4% Q2, 4.3% Q3 peak); unemp 5.3% Q1. RBNZ 2.25%; May was a 3-3 split decided by Governor Breman's casting vote. Weekend reprice: OIS collapsed to <30% for Jul 8 as oil ~$70 vs the ~$100 May assumption guts urgency.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 REPRICED: hold near-certain Wednesday (possibly by consensus) with ASB/Westpac/ANZ pointing at September lift-off + one more in Dec - H2 tightening intact, the question is how hawkish the hold sounds. Risk: a surprise hike (flagged-move credibility) squeezes NZD hard; a flat statement flushes the residual ~30% premium. Next RBNZ Jul 8.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core up to 2.5%
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%; energy 8.7% from 10.8%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Two-handed flash: the cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected), but rising core keeps a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on the core print squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: Saudi at 90% of baseline; glut intact
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; jobs flat since Jan, wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold. WTI ~$68.5-69 / Brent ~$71-72; Saudi exports back to 90% of pre-war baseline, Iranian crude stacking at sea.
strength.forward: 🔻 Glut intact into the new week and CAD lagged even Friday's bounce - structurally offered; the energy-CPI floor erodes while the terms-of-trade hit deepens; BoC sidelined Jul 15, Jul 10 jobs the only near catalyst. Risk: a funeral-week Hormuz incident re-spikes oil; a Jul 10 jobs surprise. Next BoC Jul 15.

geopolitics: Funeral week, day two - and a new dated tail. Khamenei's rites run July 4-9 across Tehran, Qom and Mashhad (15-20 million mourners expected, the largest state funeral in Iran's history), with mourners chanting for revenge and the Doha talks paused until it ends; Qatar says the concluded round made 'positive progress' and talks resume 'at the earliest possible time,' while Trump framed the pause as Iran getting 'a week off.' The substantive weekend development: Iran's ambassador to China said Tehran will DEFINITELY charge fees on Strait of Hormuz transits - with 'special treatment' for friendly nations - once the 60-day free-transit window in the ceasefire deal lapses, an idea Washington has already rejected and was actively negotiating away in Doha. That converts the toll dispute from rumor into a dated collision point (~late August) that oil is not yet pricing. For now the glut rules: WTI ~$68.5-69, Brent ~$71-72 into Friday's close, Saudi exports back to 90% of the pre-war baseline, UAE restored, Iraq tentatively recovering, Iranian crude stacking at sea, record Russian exports. Transmission unchanged: falling oil -> eroding energy-CPI floors -> less CB hawkishness at the margin (it just repriced the RBNZ from hike to hold) -> CAD holds the bag. Falling oil remains the GER40 tailwind and does NOT buy a Fed cut (US core runs 2.9%; the disinflation is energy-led). The tail remains the vacuum itself: frozen diplomacy through July 9, high emotion, a standing Hormuz routing warning - an incident with negotiators sidelined would re-spike oil into a market that has fully crushed the war premium (rescuing CAD, hurting AUD/NZD/GER40, handing USD and JPY competing haven bids). High floating storage means even a spike likely fades faster than prior episodes.
